title: Beilschmiedia cuneata
taxonomy: Beilschmiedia cuneata is a species of plant that belongs to the family Lauraceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the laurel family. The Lauraceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world and consists of approximately 2,000 species. Beilschmiedia cuneata is a plant species that is endemic to New Caledonia, an island territory in the Pacific Ocean. Classification of the plant into a genus and species level within the Lauraceae family is a matter of ongoing research and classification.

intro: Beilschmiedia cuneata, also known as the Red Sand Rosewood, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Lauraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Beilschmiedia cuneata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
